description_long: This bonsai features a dense, lush canopy of green leaves, with multiple slender trunks and prominent aerial roots extending downward, reminiscent of a miniature banyan or fig tree. The foliage forms a broad, rounded crown, providing a sense of fullness and maturity. The trunks are relatively thin and straight, with some gentle curves and naturalistic branching, giving the tree a realistic, aged appearance. The aerial roots hang freely, adding visual interest and complexity to the lower portion of the tree. The ground beneath the bonsai is scattered with fallen leaves, enhancing the naturalistic effect. No pot is visible, suggesting the focus is entirely on the tree's organic form.
description: A lush, multi-trunked bonsai with dense green foliage and hanging aerial roots, forming a broad, rounded canopy. The tree exudes a natural, mature look with scattered leaves at its base.
description_view_0: The bonsai is seen with its dense canopy and several visible aerial roots, with the trunks partially obscured by foliage.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the broad, rounded canopy and reveals more of the aerial roots and trunk structure beneath the foliage.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the verticality of the trunks and the hanging roots, with the dense foliage forming a continuous crown above.
description_view_3: From this perspective, the bonsai's canopy appears slightly more open, with the aerial roots and trunks more clearly visible.
attribute_pot_type: No pot is visible; the bonsai appears to be displayed without a container, focusing solely on the tree and its roots.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunks are mostly straight with some gentle, naturalistic curves, resembling an informal upright style with multiple stems and visible aerial roots.
attribute_branch_arrangement: Branches are densely arranged, forming a broad, rounded canopy with a natural, layered structure. The branching is irregular but balanced, contributing to a full silhouette.
attribute_leaf_density: The foliage is dense and lush, with minimal gaps, creating a thick, continuous canopy across the top of the tree.
